title: To The Generous Reader.
content:
See and not see, and if thou chance t'espy
    Some aberrations in my poetry,
    Wink at small faults; the greater, ne'ertheless,
    Hide, and with them their father's nakedness.
    Let's do our best, our watch and ward to keep;
    Homer himself, in a long work, may sleep.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "To The Generous Reader.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
